Abdominal pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the abdominal region.
Abdominal pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL

Colitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the colon.
Colitis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Colitis.Grade 2: Abdominal pain; mucus or blood in stool

Colonic hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the colon.
Colonic hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization

Diarrhea.Definition: A disorder characterized by an increase in frequency and/or loose or watery bowel movements.
Diarrhea.Grade 1: Increase of <4 stools per day over baseline; mild increase in ostomy output compared to baseline
Diarrhea.Grade 2: Increase of 4 - 6 stools per day over baseline; moderate increase in ostomy output compared to baseline; limiting instrumental ADL
Diarrhea.Grade 3: Increase of >=7 stools per day over baseline; hospitalization indicated; severe increase in ostomy output compared to baseline; limiting self care ADL

Enterocolitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the small and large intestines.
Enterocolitis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Enterocolitis.Grade 2: Abdominal pain; mucus or blood in stool
Enterocolitis.Grade 3: Severe or persistent abdominal pain; fever; ileus; peritoneal signs

Gastritis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the stomach.
Gastritis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Gastritis.Grade 2: Symptomatic; altered GI function; medical intervention indicated
Gastritis.Grade 3: Severely altered eating or gastric function; TPN or hospitalization indicated
Gastritis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent operative intervention indicated

Proctitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the rectum.
Proctitis.Grade 2: Symptomatic (e.g., rectal discomfort, passing blood or mucus); medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Proctitis.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; fecal urgency or stool incontinence; limiting self care ADL

Rectal hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the rectal wall and discharged from the anus.
Rectal hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
